claim: Two DIFFERENT parties hold two DIFFERENT guarantees over an authored SpecLink, and the boundary between them shall NEVER be blurred: the ENGINE holds the STRUCTURAL floor -- a cited symbol or test really exists, resolves by name against the domain's own authored spec/ tree, is not t.Log-only or otherwise empty (has teeth), is not an unconditional t.Skip, is not formally reused across unrelated requirements, and is not orphaned by a later code change -- while a MIRROR AUDIT, performed by a human or an LLM reading the pair (requirement claim text, authored code+test) without domain-specific heuristics baked into the engine, certifies that the cited test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim. The engine's structural checks shall NEVER be represented, documented, or relied upon as a substitute for the mirror audit's semantic certification, and the mirror audit's semantic judgment shall NEVER be encoded as a lexical or keyword heuristic inside the graph invariant layer.
why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §6 ("Каталог механических проверок") states this boundary as its closing paragraph, calling it out explicitly as an "honesty boundary": "Граница честности: движок держит СТРУКТУРНЫЙ пол. Он не может механически доказать, что тест семантически доказывает требование -- это остаётся зеркальным аудитом." R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked (#223) already implements and names the six structural checks (symbol/test resolvable, has-teeth, no-skip, no-unrelated-reuse) and its own WHY paragraph already states the boundary in passing ("HONESTY BOUNDARY (§6): these checks hold the structural floor only... they do NOT and cannot verify that a test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim; that remains a mirror audit"). This requirement promotes that boundary from a parenthetical inside #223's WHY text to its OWN first-class graph node, because the boundary is a distinct discipline concept with its own failure mode worth naming and citing independently: the session's own history shows repeated near-misses where lexical/keyword heuristics were proposed as a shortcut for semantic judgment (e.g. the CONFRONT opposite-marker gate's own false-positive collisions, R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked's own history entry documents one on 2026-07-16) -- the temptation to fold semantic certification into the engine as "just one more check_*" is the exact failure this requirement stands against. PLAN §1 documents the concrete cost of skipping this boundary in the old generator era ("t.Log('no structural atom') in 10 of 22 requirements... a always-green test is not coverage; it creates the appearance of an executable requirement") -- the structural floor prevents THAT specific failure (a hollow always-green test), but does not and cannot prevent a technically-real, technically-passing test that simply tests the wrong thing; only a mirror audit catches that. Naming both halves as one requirement (not two) reflects that the discipline IS the boundary between them, not either half alone. F5 remediation (2026-07-17, @fh adversarial review): the previous verified_by carrier (TestCheckVerifiedByTestHasTeeth_MUTATION_VacuousRoundTrip) proved only ONE structural check's own break->fix round trip (vacuous-body detection) -- one brick, not the wall this claim is actually about (the STRUCTURAL-vs-MIRROR boundary itself). The new verified_by carrier, TestStructuralFloorDoesNotCatchSemanticMismatch (internal/invariants/authored_links_test.go), proves the boundary operationally: a requirement claiming Risk-ownership validation is wired to a verified_by test that is completely unrelated (a bare arithmetic assertion) yet passes every structural check the engine has and every enforced_by/authored gate -- the FULL AllViolations sweep reports ZERO violations for a pairing no mirror audit would ever accept. That gap IS the claim: the engine cannot and does not catch this, which is exactly what 'shall never be relied upon as a substitute for the mirror audit' asserts. implemented_by keeps checkVerifiedByTestHasTeeth (internal/invariants/authored_links.go) as the STRUCTURAL-floor half's own embodiment (one of the six mechanical checks the ENGINE side of the boundary actually performs), now paired with a verified_by that demonstrates the boundary rather than one check's internal correctness.
evidence: cd D:/ai_dev/prat/HotamSpec && go test ./internal/invariants/... -run TestStructuralFloorDoesNotCatchSemanticMismatch -v : PASS (F5 remediation, 2026-07-17); the test wires a Risk-ownership-validation requirement to a semantically-unrelated (arithmetic) verified_by test that is nonetheless structurally perfect (resolves, has teeth, not vacuous, not skipped, not reused, actually compiles+passes via check_verified_by_test_passes/F1's execution gate) and asserts the FULL AllViolations sweep still reports 0 violations -- proving the structural floor cannot and does not catch this class of semantic mismatch, which only a mirror audit can
